3.why: We need the size to loop over the level-order layout.
3.invariant: values holds the tree in level order, null for gaps.

4.happening: Creating an empty list of nodes.
4.why: This list is what the stage draws as the tree.

5.happening: Creating an empty by_id lookup map.
5.why: So we can fetch any node by its id in O(1).

6.why: state_by_id[i] tells the animation how to shade node i.

7.happening: Looping to give every slot a starting state.
7.invariant: state_by_id is being filled with zeros.

8.why: Idle is the neutral, un-highlighted look.
8.invariant: All states so far are idle.

9.happening: The child walker starts at slot 1.
9.why: Slot 0 is the root; its children begin after it.
9.invariant: child points at the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Second pass: building a node for each real slot.
10.why: Turning the flat array into linked node dicts.
10.invariant: Nodes are created in level order.

11.why: Only real values become tree nodes.

11.happening: Slot {i} is null — no node here.
11.why: A null marks a missing child in the layout.

12.why: Its left/right start as None until linked.
12.invariant: The node's id equals its array index.

13.happening: Slot {child} is available for the left child.
13.why: We only read children while slots remain.

13.happening: No slots left — this node has no left child.
13.why: We are past the end of the array.

14.happening: Slot {child} = {values[child]} is a real left child.
14.why: A non-null slot means a genuine child link.

14.happening: Slot {child} is null — no left child.
14.why: A null child slot means this branch is empty.

15.why: Records the left edge for the drawn tree.
15.invariant: The left subtree hangs off this node.

16.happening: Advancing the child walker past the left slot.
16.why: The next slot belongs to the right child.

17.happening: Slot {child} is available for the right child.
17.why: We only read children while slots remain.

17.happening: No slots left — this node has no right child.
17.why: We are past the end of the array.

18.happening: Slot {child} = {values[child]} is a real right child.
18.why: A non-null slot means a genuine child link.

18.happening: Slot {child} is null — no right child.
18.why: A null child slot means this branch is empty.

19.why: Records the right edge for the drawn tree.
19.invariant: The right subtree hangs off this node.

20.happening: Advancing the child walker past the right slot.
20.why: Ready to build the next node's children.

21.happening: Adding node {i} to the drawn node list.
21.why: Once linked, the node joins the visible tree.

23.why: Both searches start from the root.
23.invariant: root points at the top of the tree.

24.happening: Preparing an empty list for p's path.
24.why: The DFS will fill it with root-to-p node ids.

25.happening: Starting a DFS from the root to find value {p}.
25.why: We need the exact chain of nodes from the root down to p.
25.invariant: path_p will end holding root → … → p.

26.happening: Looping to clear all colour states.
26.why: The second search should start on a clean tree.
26.invariant: p's path is already stored in path_p.

27.why: Wipes the highlighting left by the first search.
27.invariant: path_p is untouched by this reset.

28.happening: Preparing an empty list for q's path.
28.why: The DFS will fill it with root-to-q node ids.

29.happening: Starting a DFS from the root to find value {q}.
29.why: We need the exact chain of nodes from the root down to q.
29.invariant: path_q will end holding root → … → q.

30.happening: Looping to clear all colour states again.
30.why: So we can repaint the two finished paths cleanly.
30.invariant: Both path_p and path_q are now known.

31.why: Wipes the highlighting left by the second search.

32.happening: Painting every node on p's path.
32.why: So the learner sees the full route to p.

33.happening: Tinting node {path_p[i]} as being on p's path.
33.invariant: This node lies between the root and p.

34.happening: Painting every node on q's path.
34.why: So the learner sees the full route to q.

35.happening: Tinting node {path_q[i]} as being on q's path.
35.invariant: This node lies between the root and q.

36.why: It will be overwritten with the shared ancestor's value.

37.why: No shared node has been identified yet.

38.why: We compare the two paths from the root downward.

39.happening: Both paths still have a node at depth {depth} — compare them.
39.why: We can only match while both paths extend this deep.
39.invariant: The shared trunk is the matched prefix so far.

39.happening: One path has ended — the shared trunk is complete.
39.why: A node can be its own ancestor, so the shorter path stops us.
39.invariant: lca now holds the deepest shared node's value.

40.happening: p's path node at depth {depth} is {path_p[depth]}.
40.why: We will compare it with q's node at the same depth.

41.happening: q's path node at depth {depth} is {path_q[depth]}.
41.why: We compare it with p's node at the same depth.

42.happening: Both paths pass through the same node {a} — it is shared.
42.why: A node on both paths is a common ancestor of p and q.
42.invariant: The trunk still holds both targets below it.

42.happening: p's path goes to {a} but q's goes to {b} — they diverge here.
42.why: This is where the two routes split for good.
42.invariant: The last shared node was the LCA.

43.happening: Marking shared node {a} as part of the common trunk.
43.why: It is an ancestor of both p and q.
43.invariant: The trunk grows one node deeper.

44.happening: Remembering node {a} as the lowest shared ancestor so far.
44.why: Each deeper match overrides the previous candidate.
44.invariant: lca_id tracks the deepest shared node.

45.happening: Recording node {a}'s value as the current LCA candidate.
45.why: If the paths diverge next, this value is the answer.
45.invariant: lca updates on the following step.

46.happening: Stepping one level deeper on both paths.
46.why: Keep matching as long as the routes agree.

48.happening: Breaking out — the paths have diverged.
48.why: No node below the split can be common to both.
48.invariant: lca is fixed at the last shared value.

49.why: It is the lowest node that is an ancestor of both p and q.

52.happening: Walked off the tree — this branch is a dead end.
52.why: There is no node here to match the target.
We must check it and, if needed, its children.
52.invariant: node_id is a real node on the current path.

53.why: Nothing to find down a missing child.
53.invariant: The caller will try another direction.

54.happening: Adding node {node_id} to the current DFS path.
54.why: Tentatively assume it is on the route to the target.

55.happening: Highlighting node {node_id} as visited on this path.
55.why: The animation shows the live descent.
55.invariant: Nodes on the current path glow.

56.happening: Checking whether node {node_id}'s value equals target {target}.
56.why: If it matches, the path from the root to here is the answer path.
56.invariant: We stop descending once the target is hit.

57.happening: Node {node_id} IS the target — reporting success.
57.why: The current path now runs from the root to the target.

58.why: We search the left subtree before the right.
58.invariant: left is a child id or None.

59.why: Explore the left subtree for the target.
59.invariant: The path may grow deeper on the left.

60.happening: The target was found on the left — success.
60.why: No need to search the right subtree.
60.invariant: The path stays intact up to the target.

61.why: The left subtree did not contain the target.
61.invariant: right is a child id or None.

62.why: Explore the right subtree for the target.
62.invariant: The path may grow deeper on the right.

63.happening: The target was found on the right — success.
63.why: The path down through the right subtree is kept.
63.invariant: The path stays intact up to the target.

64.happening: Neither subtree held the target — removing node {node_id}.
64.why: It is not on the route to the target, so we backtrack.
64.invariant: path shrinks back to the parent.

65.invariant: This node is off the current path.

66.happening: Reporting failure so the caller keeps searching.
66.why: The target is not under this node.

init.happening: Rebuilding the BST from its level-order array.
init.why: We need the linked tree before searching it.
init.invariant: Setup runs once before the searches.

recurse.happening: Descending into a subtree via a recursive DFS call.
recurse.why: Depth-first search locates a target node's path.
recurse.invariant: The call stack mirrors the current path.

update.happening: Updating path bookkeeping or a node's colour state.
update.why: So the animation reflects the search's progress.

loop.happening: Comparing the two paths at the current depth.
loop.why: The last matching depth marks the LCA.
loop.invariant: The matched prefix is the shared trunk.

check.happening: Testing a node against the target or the other path.
check.why: The comparison decides the next move.
check.invariant: Decisions never use the BST ordering here.

move.happening: Advancing the DFS pointer or backtracking.
move.why: We explore or retreat along the tree.
move.invariant: The path grows or shrinks accordingly.

return.happening: Returning a result up the recursion or the final LCA.
return.why: Success or failure propagates to the caller.
return.invariant: The answer is settled when the top call returns.
